headline: General Mills Hits Color Free School Goal As Stock Weakness Persists
summary: General Mills has fully removed certified colors from its K-12 school foods portfolio ahead of schedule. The change covers all school-focused products, including reformulated versions of popular items such as Lucky Charms. This shift aligns with growing attention on food additives in children's diets and school meal quality standards. For investors watching NYSE:GIS, this move comes as the stock trades at $43.57, with a 1 year return of a 26.5% decline and a 3 year return of a 37.7%...
